city-context.epithets: Jewel of the North; the City That Never Sleeps; the Shining City (pre-disaster)

section.e: Dagult Neverember; self-proclaimed rightful heir to the Neverwinter throne (contested); rules as Lord Protector; simultaneously Open Lord of Waterdeep until removed; currently focused on Neverwinter reconstruction
Neverember's elite personal guard+enforcers; named for the legendary nine heroes of old Neverwinter; function as secret police as much as bodyguards; widely mistrusted by citizens
Neverember's hereditary claim is disputed; no surviving member of the old Alagondar noble line acknowledged; many citizens resent autocratic rule while accepting it as necessary for reconstruction
Political faction supporting Neverember's rebuilding project; loyal to the Lord Protector; often wealthy newcomers who arrived after the disaster and have no attachment to the old city
Resistance faction; claim to defend old Neverwinter's independence; see Neverember as an occupier; conduct sporadic sabotage; popular sympathy among long-term residents

River Neverwinter stays warm year-round due to geothermal vents upstream; never freezes; gives the city its name; source of the city's unique climate advantage; vital for winter trade when other northern rivers ice over
Main restored district; administrative center,markets,docks; Neverember's government operates here; safest part of the city; where visitors typically stay
Formerly wealthy residential district; damaged in disaster; partially restored; wealthier citizens+Neverember loyalists moving back in; still has abandoned sections
Northern district; largely ruined; dangerous due to remnant monsters and structural instability; Sons of Alagondar use ruins for hideouts; officially off-limits but unofficially entered constantly
Forest to the east; corrupted by the disaster's magical fallout; plagued beasts,undead,and stranger things; not a safe place to enter without preparation
Massive rift that tore open in the city during the disaster; sealed/bridged by 1492 but the area remains unstable and psychically disturbing; a vivid reminder of the catastrophe
Volcano to the NE; erupted ~1451 DR; pyroclastic flows destroyed much of the city; thousands killed; triggered the Chasm opening; the defining event for all current residents
Spellplague 1385 DR already weakened the city before Hotenow; combination of planar disruptions+volcanic destruction left the city near-abandoned for decades

Neverember began serious rebuilding ~1467 DR; brought in workers,soldiers,merchants from Waterdeep and elsewhere; city is functional but far from its former glory; ongoing construction visible everywhere
Significantly smaller than pre-disaster; mix of original survivors,returning former residents,and new arrivals attracted by cheap land+opportunity; social tensions between old-timers and newcomers
The old castle; partially rebuilt; Neverember uses it as his seat; some wings still ruined; legendary for its size and former glory; adventurers explore unreconstructed sections (unofficially)
Tyr's temple; restored early in reconstruction; serves as courts+civic center; one of the most intact major buildings; Neverember uses its legitimacy for official functions
Main market in the Enclave; largest concentration of active merchants; prices slightly higher than comparable Sword Coast cities (supply logistics); most reliable source of standard goods
Popular tavern in Protector's Enclave; gathering point for adventurers+mercenaries; job board; Neverember's agents known to recruit here; owner: Madame Rosene
Along the Neverwinter River; warehouses,fishing operations,small boat traffic; less affected by disaster than interior districts; working-class,practical,mistrustful of outsiders
Neverember's city guard; funded by Waterdeep gold via the Lord Protector; reasonably paid and reasonably disciplined; loyalty is to Neverember not the city; not a city Watch in traditional sense
Mercenaries from Mintarn island hired by Neverember to supplement the guard; competent but seen as foreign occupiers by old-time residents; rotating contracts mean no permanent loyalty
Harpers active in Neverwinter; concerned about Neverember's consolidation of power; also monitoring Blighted Witchwood and Underdark connections under the city; operate quietly
Massive building demand=jobs for laborers,craftsmen,merchants; wages slightly above Sword Coast average due to demand; Neverember spending Waterdeep treasury gold to fund growth
Harbor rebuilt and operational; less traffic than pre-disaster but growing; river advantage means it functions year-round when northern ports are iced; strategic value acknowledged by Lords' Alliance
Property prices low relative to Waterdeep or Baldur's Gate; opportunity for those willing to accept risk and ongoing instability; Neverember grants land to attract settlers
Some goods harder to find than in larger cities; magical items especially limited; adventurers wanting exotic equipment often need to commission or travel to Waterdeep
Residents who stayed through or returned after the disaster have a particular hardness and civic pride; not easily impressed; distrust easy promises; respect demonstrated competence
Open support for Sons of Alagondar can lead to arrest by Neverwinter Nine; open criticism of Neverember is risky; most citizens navigate with careful neutrality; tavern conversations guarded
Unusually welcoming; reconstruction creates constant demand for cleared ruins,explored threats,recovered artifacts; adventurers are economically valuable here in ways they aren't elsewhere
Despite being a named city, Neverwinter in 1492 has a frontier quality; rules are enforced selectively; the Watch's reach doesn't extend everywhere; self-reliance valued
